coach_note: Race week. Bristol Half Marathon on Sunday. The build has gone well — 28 runs in the last 30 days totalling ~195 miles, including the key 4×2k at 5:35/mi, a strong 12-mile sub-threshold session (load 83), Amsterdam EIM sharpeners, and a 3×2k on Saturday. CTL is 37-38, resting HR stable at 39-45bpm, HRV solid (53-67). Sleep averaging 83/100. The body is in good shape. Taper this week: easy runs Mon-Wed, strides Thursday, shakeout Friday, rest Saturday. Race plan: controlled start at 5:48-5:50, settle into 5:44 from mile 4, bring it home at 5:40-5:35. Negative split. Trust the fitness.

predictions.rationale: 195 miles in the last 30 days with consistent quality. 4×2k at 5:35/mi handled well, 12-mile sub-threshold session at load 83 confirms aerobic fitness. Amsterdam EIM sessions kept the legs sharp. CTL stable at 38, resting HR and HRV normal. Flat Bristol course is ideal. Target is realistic.
PB 16:11 set during marathon training with no 5k-specific work. The HM block has built a bigger aerobic engine. With dedicated 5k speed work over summer at Battersea, sub-16 is realistic.
